intro: Practice speaking about birds (revision draft) through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Some birds migrate.
Some birds migrate every autumn.
Some birds migrate every autumn to warmer regions.
Some birds migrate every autumn to warmer regions where food is easier to find.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Birds need food.
Birds need food during winter.
Birds need food during winter because insects become scarce.
Birds need food during winter because insects become scarce in cold weather.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Many birds fly together.
Many birds fly together in a V shape.
Many birds fly together in a V shape to save energy.
Many birds fly together in a V shape to save energy on long journeys.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Winters might become warmer.
Winters might become warmer in some regions.
Winters might become warmer in some regions because of climate change.
Winters might become warmer in some regions because of climate change, changing the way some birds migrate.

automaticResponses.question: Why do many birds fly in a V shape?
How might climate change affect birds?
automaticResponses.answer: It might change the way they migrate.

expansionChallenge: Some birds travel thousands of kilometers.
Some birds travel thousands of kilometers every year.
Some birds travel thousands of kilometers every year without getting lost.
Some birds travel thousands of kilometers every year without getting lost by using the Sun and the stars.
finalSpeaking: Some birds migrate every autumn to warmer regions where food is easier to find. They need food during winter because insects become scarce in cold weather. Many birds fly together in a V shape to save energy on long journeys. As the climate changes, the way some birds migrate may change in the future.

topicTitle: Why are coral reefs called the rainforests of the sea?
intro: Practice speaking about coral reefs through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Coral reefs are living ecosystems.
Coral reefs are living ecosystems in warm oceans.
Coral reefs are living ecosystems in warm oceans that support many sea animals.
Coral reefs are living ecosystems in warm oceans that support thousands of different sea animals.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Many animals depend on coral reefs.
Many animals depend on coral reefs for shelter.
Many animals depend on coral reefs for shelter and food.
Many animals depend on coral reefs for shelter and food because young sea animals can grow there safely.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Coral reefs grow slowly.
Coral reefs grow slowly over many years.
Coral reefs grow slowly over many years as tiny coral animals build them.
Coral reefs grow slowly over many years as tiny coral animals build them layer by layer.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Coral reefs might disappear.
Coral reefs might disappear in some oceans.
Coral reefs might disappear in some oceans because the water becomes warmer.
Coral reefs might disappear in some oceans because the water becomes warmer, leaving many sea animals without safe homes.

automaticResponses.question: How long does a coral reef take to grow?
What might happen if coral reefs disappear?
automaticResponses.answer: Many sea animals would lose their homes.

expansionChallenge: Many fish return to coral reefs.
Many fish return to coral reefs every day.
Many fish return to coral reefs every day to stay safe.
Many fish return to coral reefs every day to stay safe and find food among the coral.
finalSpeaking: Coral reefs are living ecosystems in warm oceans that support thousands of different sea animals. Many animals depend on coral reefs for shelter and food because young sea animals can grow there safely. Coral reefs grow slowly as tiny coral animals build them layer by layer. If coral reefs disappear because the oceans become warmer, many sea animals will lose their homes.

topicTitle: What is the difference between magma and lava?
intro: Practice speaking about volcanoes through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Volcanoes release melted rock.
Volcanoes release melted rock during eruptions.
Volcanoes release melted rock during eruptions from deep underground.
Volcanoes release melted rock during eruptions from deep underground after pressure builds for many years.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Pressure keeps increasing.
Pressure keeps increasing beneath Earth's surface.
Pressure keeps increasing beneath Earth's surface because magma rises.
Pressure keeps increasing beneath Earth's surface because magma rises and trapped gas cannot escape easily.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Magma becomes lava.
Magma becomes lava above the ground.
Magma becomes lava above the ground after an eruption.
Magma becomes lava above the ground after an eruption and slowly cools into solid rock.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: A volcano might erupt.
A volcano might erupt without warning.
A volcano might erupt without warning near a town.
A volcano might erupt without warning near a town, forcing people to leave before lava reaches the area.

automaticResponses.question: What might people do during an eruption?
automaticResponses.answer: They might leave the area quickly.

expansionChallenge: Scientists monitor volcanoes.
Scientists monitor volcanoes every day.
Scientists monitor volcanoes every day to detect changes.
Scientists monitor volcanoes every day to detect changes and warn nearby communities before an eruption.
finalSpeaking: Volcanoes release melted rock during eruptions from deep underground after pressure builds for many years. Pressure keeps increasing because magma rises and trapped gas cannot escape easily. When magma reaches the surface, it becomes lava and slowly cools into solid rock. Scientists monitor volcanoes every day to warn nearby communities before an eruption.

topicTitle: How do fossils help us understand dinosaurs?
intro: Practice speaking about dinosaurs through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Dinosaurs lived on Earth.
Dinosaurs lived on Earth millions of years ago.
Dinosaurs lived on Earth millions of years ago in many different environments.
Dinosaurs lived on Earth millions of years ago in many different environments before they became extinct.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Scientists study fossils.
Scientists study fossils to learn about dinosaurs.
Scientists study fossils to learn about dinosaurs that no longer exist.
Scientists study fossils to learn about dinosaurs that no longer exist and the world they lived in.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Bones become fossils.
Bones become fossils over a long time.
Bones become fossils over a long time under layers of rock.
Bones become fossils over a long time under layers of rock as minerals slowly replace them.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: People might discover a fossil.
People might discover a fossil in the future.
People might discover a fossil in the future that changes what we know.
People might discover a fossil in the future that changes what we know about dinosaur life.

automaticResponses.question: What might a new fossil do?
automaticResponses.answer: It might change what we know.

expansionChallenge: Every fossil tells a story.
Every fossil tells a story about the past.
Every fossil tells a story about the past that scientists can study.
Every fossil tells a story about the past that scientists can study to understand ancient life.
finalSpeaking: Dinosaurs lived on Earth millions of years ago in many different environments before they became extinct. Scientists study fossils to learn about dinosaurs and the world they lived in. Bones become fossils under layers of rock as minerals slowly replace them. A new fossil discovery could change what we know about ancient life.

topicTitle: Why does the Moon seem to change shape?
intro: Practice speaking about the moon through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: The Moon changes shape.
The Moon changes shape every month.
The Moon changes shape every month as it orbits Earth.
The Moon changes shape every month as it orbits Earth, creating different phases in the night sky.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: The Moon reflects sunlight.
The Moon reflects sunlight instead of making its own light.
The Moon reflects sunlight instead of making its own light, so we see different phases.
The Moon reflects sunlight instead of making its own light, so we see different phases as its position changes.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: The Moon moves around Earth.
The Moon moves around Earth in a regular orbit.
The Moon moves around Earth in a regular orbit about once a month.
The Moon moves around Earth in a regular orbit about once a month, changing the angle of sunlight we see.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: The Moon might disappear.
The Moon might disappear from our sky.
The Moon might disappear from our sky, changing the nights we know.
The Moon might disappear from our sky, changing the nights we know and the ocean tides around the world.

automaticResponses.question: Does the Moon make its own light?
automaticResponses.answer: No, it reflects the Sun's light.

automaticResponses.question: How long does one orbit take?
What would happen without the Moon?

expansionChallenge: People have always watched the Moon.
People have always watched the Moon with curiosity.
People have always watched the Moon with curiosity to understand the sky.
People have always watched the Moon with curiosity to understand the sky and measure the passage of time.
finalSpeaking: The Moon changes shape every month as it orbits Earth, creating different phases in the night sky. It reflects sunlight instead of making its own light, so we see different phases as its position changes. The Moon moves around Earth about once a month, changing the angle of sunlight we see. Without the Moon, the night sky and the ocean tides would be very different.

intro: Practice speaking about artificial intelligence through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Artificial intelligence learns patterns.
Artificial intelligence learns patterns from data.
Artificial intelligence learns patterns from data instead of memorizing facts.
Artificial intelligence learns patterns from data instead of memorizing facts, allowing it to make useful predictions.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Data is important.
Data is important because AI learns from it.
Data is important because AI learns from it through many examples.
Data is important because AI learns from it through many examples rather than a single answer.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: AI compares examples.
AI compares examples again and again.
AI compares examples again and again to find patterns.
AI compares examples again and again to find patterns that help it make decisions.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: AI might become more helpful.
AI might become more helpful in daily life.
AI might become more helpful in daily life if people use it wisely.
AI might become more helpful in daily life if people use it wisely while continuing to think critically.

automaticResponses.question: How should people use AI?
automaticResponses.answer: They should use it wisely and think critically.

expansionChallenge: Teachers can use AI.
Teachers can use AI to save time.
Teachers can use AI to save time when preparing lessons.
Teachers can use AI to save time when preparing lessons and spend more time helping students.
finalSpeaking: Artificial intelligence learns patterns from data instead of memorizing facts, allowing it to make useful predictions. Data is important because AI learns from many examples rather than a single answer. AI compares examples again and again to find patterns that help it make decisions. People can benefit from AI most when they use it wisely while continuing to think critically.

intro: Practice speaking about libraries through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Libraries collect knowledge.
Libraries collect knowledge for everyone.
Libraries collect knowledge for everyone in many different forms.
Libraries collect knowledge for everyone in many different forms, from printed books to digital resources.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Libraries are important.
Libraries are important because they provide free access.
Libraries are important because they provide free access to information.
Libraries are important because they provide free access to information and lifelong learning opportunities.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: People visit libraries.
People visit libraries to study.
People visit libraries to study and do research.
People visit libraries to study and do research in a quiet place where they can focus.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Libraries might disappear.
Libraries might disappear from some cities.
Libraries might disappear from some cities if people stop using them.
Libraries might disappear from some cities if people stop using them, reducing equal access to knowledge.

automaticResponses.question: Why are libraries important?
automaticResponses.answer: They provide free access to information.

automaticResponses.question: What can people do there?
automaticResponses.answer: They can study and do research.

automaticResponses.question: What might happen without libraries?
automaticResponses.answer: Equal access to knowledge could decrease.

expansionChallenge: A library opens doors.
A library opens doors to learning.
A library opens doors to learning for every generation.
A library opens doors to learning for every generation, regardless of age or background.
finalSpeaking: Libraries collect knowledge in many different forms, from printed books to digital resources. They are important because they provide free access to information and lifelong learning opportunities. People visit libraries to study and do research in a quiet place where they can focus. Without libraries, equal access to knowledge could become more difficult.

intro: Practice speaking about photography through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Photographs capture moments.
Photographs capture important moments.
Photographs capture important moments that quickly pass.
Photographs capture important moments that quickly pass, allowing us to remember them later.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Memories fade.
Memories fade over time.
Memories fade over time unless we revisit them.
Memories fade over time unless we revisit them through stories or photographs.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: A camera records light.
A camera records light through a lens.
A camera records light through a lens to create an image.
A camera records light through a lens to create an image that preserves a moment in time.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Old photos might disappear.
Old photos might disappear forever.
Old photos might disappear forever if they are not protected.
Old photos might disappear forever if they are not protected, taking valuable family memories with them.

automaticResponses.question: How does a camera work?
automaticResponses.answer: It records light through a lens.

automaticResponses.question: What might happen to old photos?

expansionChallenge: One picture tells a story.
One picture tells a meaningful story.
One picture tells a meaningful story without many words.
One picture tells a meaningful story without many words because images can express powerful emotions.
finalSpeaking: Photographs capture important moments that quickly pass, allowing us to remember them later. Memories fade over time unless we revisit them through stories or photographs. A camera records light through a lens to create an image that preserves a moment in time. Protecting old photographs helps preserve family memories for future generations.

topicTitle: How has the Internet changed communication?
intro: Practice speaking about the internet through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: The Internet connects people.
The Internet connects people instantly.
The Internet connects people instantly across long distances.
The Internet connects people instantly across long distances through messages, videos, and shared information.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Information spreads quickly.
Information spreads quickly online.
Information spreads quickly online because people share it.
Information spreads quickly online because millions of people share ideas every day.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: People communicate online.
People communicate online in many ways.
People communicate online in many ways using different platforms.
People communicate online in many ways using different platforms to learn, work, and build relationships.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: The Internet might stop working.
The Internet might stop working for a day.
The Internet might stop working for a day in one country.
The Internet might stop working for a day in one country, changing how people communicate and work.

automaticResponses.question: What if the Internet stopped working?

expansionChallenge: Technology changes society.
Technology changes society every day.
Technology changes society every day by connecting people.
Technology changes society every day by connecting people and making information easier to share.
finalSpeaking: The Internet connects people instantly across long distances through messages, videos, and shared information. Information spreads quickly online because millions of people share ideas every day. People communicate using different platforms to learn, work, and build relationships. If the Internet stopped working for a day, communication would become much more difficult.

intro: Practice speaking about economy through expanding patterns.

patterns.name: What
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Prices change.
Prices change every day.
Prices change every day in markets.
Prices change every day in markets as people buy and sell goods.

patterns.name: Why
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the Why pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Demand increases.
Demand increases when more people want something.
Demand increases when more people want something than stores can provide.
Demand increases when more people want something than stores can provide, causing prices to rise.

patterns.name: How
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the How pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Markets balance supply and demand.
Markets balance supply and demand through prices.
Markets balance supply and demand through prices that change over time.
Markets balance supply and demand through prices that change over time as buyers and sellers make decisions.

patterns.name: What if
patterns.drillInstruction: Practice the What if pattern by expanding each sentence step by step.
steps: Demand might fall.
Demand might fall after interest decreases.
Demand might fall after interest decreases and fewer people buy a product.
Demand might fall after interest decreases and fewer people buy a product, encouraging sellers to lower prices.

automaticResponses.question: When do prices usually rise?
automaticResponses.answer: When demand becomes higher than supply.

automaticResponses.question: What might happen if demand falls?

expansionChallenge: People make choices.
People make choices every day.
People make choices every day with limited money.
People make choices every day with limited money, influencing the economy little by little.
finalSpeaking: Prices change every day in markets as people buy and sell goods. When demand becomes higher than supply, prices usually rise. Markets balance supply and demand through prices that change as buyers and sellers make decisions. Every choice people make with their money influences the economy little by little.
